Hr. Darryl ~\Jhite 
Secretary of the Senate 
State Capitol 
Sacramento, 
C~. 95814 
Dear Nr. VJhite: 
Thank you for your recent letter to the Com~ission 
which asked fo~ an interpretation of Gov. Code §810l2. 
In 
particular, you have asked what is the meaning of "if at 
least 40 days prior to passage of each house, the bill in 
its final form has been delivered to the Commission." 
It has been the interpretation of the staff that any 
bill amending the Political Reform Act of 1974 must be in 
its last. amended form et least 40 days prior to the Li-.nal 
passage in each house of the Legislature. 
Thus, a bill may 
be passed initially by the Senate prior to the 40-day period 
if it is returned to the Senate for another vote after the 
40-da1 period. 
Under legislative rules, bills which are 
amended in the Assembly must be retcrned to the Senate for 
concurrence. 
If the Assembly passes the bill 40 days after 
it has been amended and sent to the Commission and the 
amended bill is returned to the Senate for concurrence, 
there has been compliance with §81012 even if the Se~ate 
initially voted on the bill prior to the 40-day requirement. 
Finally, the followi~g procedure also would copply with 
the Political Reform Act: 
A bill could be passed by the 
Senate prior to the 40-day period, passed by the AsseAbly 
after the 40-day period, and then returned to the Senate for 
a revote even though the bill was not amended in the 
Assernbly. 
If you have any questions, please contact Robert Stern, 
the Commission's General Counsel.

sections.question: What is the meaning of 'if at least 40 days prior to passage of each house, the bill in its final form has been delivered to the Commission' under Gov. Code §81012?
sections.conclusion: It has been the interpretation of the staff that any bill amending the Political Reform Act of 1974 must be in its last amended form at least 40 days prior to the final passage in each house of the Legislature. A bill may be passed initially by the Senate prior to the 40-day period if it is returned to the Senate for another vote after the 40-day period. Compliance is achieved when amended bills are returned to the Senate for concurrence at least 40 days after being sent to the Commission, even if the Senate initially voted prior to the 40-day requirement.
sections.question_synthetic: The Secretary of the Senate requests an interpretation of Government Code Section 81012's 40-day requirement for bills amending the Political Reform Act, specifically what constitutes compliance with the language about delivering bills in their final form to the Commission.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC interprets the 40-day requirement to mean bills must be in their final amended form at least 40 days before final passage in each legislative house. Compliance is satisfied when bills amended in the Assembly are returned to the Senate for concurrence at least 40 days after submission to the Commission, or when bills pass both houses in sequence with the second house acting 40 days after the bill was delivered to the Commission.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains both the incoming request and the FPPC response letter. The response letter is the main advice letter. Minor OCR errors present ('SFNATE' for 'SENATE', 'Li-.nal' for 'final'). Conclusion section synthesized from body paragraphs as no formal conclusion header exists.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: What is the meaning of 'if at least 40 days prior to passage of each house, the bill in its final form has been delivered to the Commission' under Gov. Code §81012?

CONCLUSION: It has been the interpretation of the staff that any bill amending the Political Reform Act of 1974 must be in its last amended form at least 40 days prior to the final passage in each house of the Legislature. A bill may be passed initially by the Senate prior to the 40-day period if it is returned to the Senate for another vote after the 40-day period. Compliance is achieved when amended bills are returned to the Senate for concurrence at least 40 days after being sent to the Commission, even if the Senate initially voted prior to the 40-day requirement.

embedding.summary: The FPPC responds to the Secretary of the Senate's inquiry regarding the interpretation of Government Code §81012's 40-day requirement for bills amending the Political Reform Act. The response provides guidance on when bills must be in final form and explains how the 40-day period applies across multiple legislative votes and amendments.
